title: Top Motorcycle Exporter China Urges Industry to Curb Price Wars
summary: China’s motorcycle industry trade body urged companies across the sector to curb price wars and focus on higher-quality growth, warning that imitation and a flood of similar products are squeezing profits and hurting the reputation of Chinese manufacturing.

title: Former ECB Rate-Setter Muller Reports for Duty as Volunteer Cop
summary: Just days after finishing a seven-year stint at the helm of Estonia’s central bank, Madis Muller is preparing for a very different role: volunteering in the police’s criminal-investigations unit.

title: Prices Likely to Stay Higher Even If Conflict Ends, ECB’s Nagel Says
summary: Prices are likely to stay elevated for longer even if the war in Iran were to end soon, Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel said in an interview with Deutschlandfunk.

title: Switzerland Asks If 10 Million People Is Where to Draw the Line
summary: A Swiss plebiscite this weekend on whether to impose a population ceiling is offering voters a choice that threatens to become their most consequential yet of the current century.

title: US Strikes Iranian Drones While Talks Continue for Interim Deal
summary: The US shot down Iranian drones near the Strait of Hormuz as talks continued for an interim peace deal to reopen the strategic waterway, adding to uncertainty over when an agreement can be reached.

title: Korean Media Giant JoongAng’s JTBC Defaults, Downgraded to Junk
summary: JTBC Co. Ltd., the broadcaster owned by South Korea’s media giant JoongAng Group, defaulted on securitized loans and was downgraded to junk by a local ratings firm, triggering a series of rating cuts across the conglomerate.

title: Geely to Shut Some Units, Focus on HK-Listed Arm, Chairman Says
summary: China’s Zhejiang Geely Holding Group will streamline its structure by shutting down or merging some entities and concentrate resources around its Hong Kong-listed arm, in a bid to improve governance, Chairman Li Shufu said.

title: Challenges and Opportunities of Investing in African Startups
summary: Investment into African startups held up in 2025, even as funding conditions tightened. According to AVCA, more than $5 billion was invested in African startups last year, with deal activity also rising. Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja spoke with venture capital investor Andrew Firman from Kaleo Ventures on opportunities and challenges of investing in the continent. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Kills Tren de Aragua Leader Guerrero in Strike on Compound
summary: The US killed the leader of the Tren De Aragua drug cartel, Niño Guerrero, in a strike that was carried out with the assistance of Venezuela, President Donald Trump said.

title: China Rebukes US Over ‘Military’ Labeling of Its Top Firms
summary: China’s Commerce Ministry said it firmly opposes the US decision to label additional firms as “military companies,” accusing Washington of using national security as a pretext to curb the development of Chinese firms.

title: Inside SpaceX and Elon Musk’s $75 Billion IPO
summary: SpaceX’s first day on the stock market transformed the startup into one of the world’s most-valuable public companies, handed buyers of the IPO a 19% return and turned its founder Elon Musk into the world’s first trillionaire. Ed Ludlow reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Why Musk Raced to Take SpaceX Public in the World’s Biggest IPO
summary: It was meant to happen once humans were regularly flying to Mars. Then the AI boom took off.

title: From London to Sydney, Traders Ready to Watch SpaceX’s Debut
summary: Investors and retail traders from across the US and around the world were glued to their screens on Friday as Elon Musk’s SpaceX started trading in the stock market after the biggest initial public offering in Wall Street history.

title: CFTC Considers Blocking CME’s 24/7 Oil Contract Bid
summary: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission is considering whether to block CME Group Inc.’s bid to launch a round-the-clock oil contract, heightening tensions between the market stalwart and its regulator.

title: Knicks' Team Sacrifices and Success Inspire New York Optimism
summary: Bill Bradley, former US Senator and two-time NBA champion, discussed the New York Knicks' recent success, highlighting how Jalen Brunson gave up millions in free agency to allow the team more financial flexibility to build a stronger roster. He emphasized that this special group's dedication and shared beliefs have led to their success, which resonates positively with New Yorkers. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: What to Know About SpaceX’s Record-Breaking IPO
summary: SpaceX had the largest stock-market debut in history when it went public on June 12. The company raised $75 billion in the initial public offering and ended its first day on the public markets with a market capitalization of around $2.2 trillion. The IPO turned SpaceX founder Elon Musk into the world’s first trillionaire.

title: SpaceX IPO Sparks Surge in Space Industry Investment and Market Optimism
summary: Heather Pringle, CEO of the Space Foundation, discussed the impact of SpaceX's historic $75 Billion IPO on the broader space industry and investment landscape. She highlighted that SpaceX's public listing marks a significant milestone for a maturing space sector, demonstrating viable pathways to profitability through communications, launch services, and navigation technologies. she speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bloomberg Money 6/12/2026
summary: Bloomberg Money takes the pulse of your financial life, powered by the reporting of our global newsroom. Chanos & Company President & Founder Jim Chanos and Former US Treasury Secretary and Columbia University Professor of International & Public Affairs Jacob Lew. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: NYC Comptroller Raises Concerns Over SpaceX Index Inclusion and Governance Structure
summary: New York City Comptroller Mark Levine discussed the fast-tracking of SpaceX's inclusion into major indexes such as MSCI Global Standard and FTSE Russell, highlighting concerns about the abandonment of traditional criteria like seasoning periods and earnings track records. Levine emphasized the unprecedented governance structure of SpaceX, where a single individual holds dominant control with limited shareholder rights and no independent board oversight. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Look Ahead: Retail Sales, FMOC, NBA Finals
summary: Bloomberg's Lisa Mateo joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Riding Global Tailwinds: Masters in Business with Jean Eric Salata
summary: Barry speaks with Jean Eric Salata, chair of EQT group. They discuss his time working in Asian private equity investment along with what he sees as necessary to become a good investor across different cultures including what he learned in Japan and Hong Kong. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Knicks Win Triggered Record Loss for Susquehanna Sports Traders
summary: Jeff Yass, the founder of the Wall Street trading firm Susquehanna International Group, has long been a New York Knicks fan. But he couldn’t fully savor the moment when the team completed its epic comeback to win game four of the NBA finals.

title: Matt Holt Said to Near $12 Billion Deal for Ensemble Health
summary: Private-equity executive Matt Holt’s investment firm Thoreau Group is in advanced talks to acquire Ensemble Health Partners in a deal valuing the company at about $12 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: SpaceX IPO Whips Musk Fans Into Frenzy
summary: Bloomberg's Nikki Waller joins Scarlet Fu and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." Shares of SpaceX climbed in their first day of trading on Friday following a $75 billion IPO that smashed records (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ice Cube: Ready To Take BIG3 to the Next Level
summary: Rapper & Co-Founder of Big3 league Ice Cube, discusses the league preparing to go public after a $290 million valuation. He discusses the early days of the league, how the fans have really embraced it, and what it means to be the first publicly traded sports league. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Randall Williams on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Chevron CEO Wirth on Energy Market Volatility
summary:
Michael K. Wirth, Chairman of the Board & CEO, Chevron Corporation discusses investment, scale and long-term strategy with Bloomberg’s Annmarie Hordern at the Bloomberg Energy Security Executive Briefing 2026 in Houston.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Space Stocks Tumble as Investors Race Toward Musk’s IPO
summary: SpaceX’s long-awaited initial public offering triggered a selloff Friday in the shares of other rocket, satellite and space-related companies.

title: Energy Market Leaders on Navigating Risk
summary: Rebecca Babin, Managing Director & Senior Energy Trader, CIBC Private Wealth and Dan Pickering, Founder & Chief Investment Officer, Pickering Energy Partners discuss volatility, diversification and returns across energy markets with Bloomberg’s Julie Fine at the Bloomberg Energy Security Executive Briefing 2026 in Houston. (Source: Bloomberg)
